# SimulationApi

All URIs are relative to *https://sandbox.cashfree.com/pg*

|Method | HTTP request | Description|
|------------- | ------------- | -------------|
|[**pGFetchSettlementSimulation**](#pgfetchsettlementsimulation) | **GET** /simulate/settlement/{simulation_id} | Fetch Settlement Simulation|
|[**pGFetchSimulation**](#pgfetchsimulation) | **GET** /simulate/{simulation_id} | Fetch Simulation|

# **pGFetchSettlementSimulation**
> SettlementSimulationResponse pGFetchSettlementSimulation()

Use this API to fetch the details of a simulated settlement.

### Example

```typescript
import {
    SimulationApi,
    Configuration
} from 'cashfree-pg';

const configuration = new Configuration();
const apiInstance = new SimulationApi(configuration);

let x_api_version: string; //API version to be used. Format is in YYYY-MM-DD. (default to '2026-01-01')
let simulation_id: string; //Provide the SimulationId for which the details have to be fetched. (default to undefined)
let x_request_id: string; //Request ID for the API call. Can be used to resolve tech issues. Communicate this in your tech related queries to Cashfree. (optional) (default to undefined)
let x_idempotency_key: string; //An idempotency key is a unique identifier you include with your API call. If the request fails or times out, you can safely retry it using the same key to avoid duplicate actions.  (optional) (default to undefined)

const { status, data } = await apiInstance.pGFetchSettlementSimulation(
    x_api_version,
    simulation_id,
    x_request_id,
    x_idempotency_key
);
```

### Parameters

|Name | Type | Description  | Notes|
|------------- | ------------- | ------------- | -------------|
| **x_api_version** | [**string**] | API version to be used. Format is in YYYY-MM-DD. | defaults to '2026-01-01'|
| **simulation_id** | [**string**] | Provide the SimulationId for which the details have to be fetched. | defaults to undefined|
| **x_request_id** | [**string**] | Request ID for the API call. Can be used to resolve tech issues. Communicate this in your tech related queries to Cashfree. | (optional) defaults to undefined|
| **x_idempotency_key** | [**string**] | An idempotency key is a unique identifier you include with your API call. If the request fails or times out, you can safely retry it using the same key to avoid duplicate actions.  | (optional) defaults to undefined|


### Return type

**SettlementSimulationResponse**

### Authorization

[XPartnerAPIKey](../README.md#XPartnerAPIKey), [XClientSecret](../README.md#XClientSecret), [XPartnerMerchantID](../README.md#XPartnerMerchantID), [XClientID](../README.md#XClientID), [XClientSignatureHeader](../README.md#XClientSignatureHeader)

### HTTP request headers

 - **Content-Type**: Not defined
 - **Accept**: application/json


### HTTP response details
| Status code | Description | Response headers |
|-------------|-------------|------------------|
|**200** | Fetched simulated response. |  * x-api-version -  <br>  * x-ratelimit-limit -  <br>  * x-ratelimit-remaining -  <br>  * x-ratelimit-retry -  <br>  * x-ratelimit-type -  <br>  * x-request-id -  <br>  |

# **pGFetchSimulation**
> SimulationResponse pGFetchSimulation()

Use this API to fetch simulated payment details.

### Example

```typescript
import {
    SimulationApi,
    Configuration
} from 'cashfree-pg';

const configuration = new Configuration();
const apiInstance = new SimulationApi(configuration);

let x_api_version: string; //API version to be used. Format is in YYYY-MM-DD. (default to '2026-01-01')
let simulation_id: string; //Provide the SimulationId for which the details have to be fetched. (default to undefined)
let x_request_id: string; //Request ID for the API call. Can be used to resolve tech issues. Communicate this in your tech related queries to Cashfree. (optional) (default to undefined)
let x_idempotency_key: string; //An idempotency key is a unique identifier you include with your API call. If the request fails or times out, you can safely retry it using the same key to avoid duplicate actions.  (optional) (default to undefined)

const { status, data } = await apiInstance.pGFetchSimulation(
    x_api_version,
    simulation_id,
    x_request_id,
    x_idempotency_key
);
```

# **pGSimulatePayment**
> SimulationResponse pGSimulatePayment(SimulateRequest)

Use this API to simulate payment. To use this API you should first create an order using the Create Order API. Also, you need to create a payment with the same order.

### Example

```typescript
import {
    SimulationApi,
    Configuration,
    SimulateRequest
} from 'cashfree-pg';

const configuration = new Configuration();
const apiInstance = new SimulationApi(configuration);

let x_api_version: string; //API version to be used. Format is in YYYY-MM-DD. (default to '2026-01-01')
let SimulateRequest: SimulateRequest; //Request body for simulation.
let x_request_id: string; //Request ID for the API call. Can be used to resolve tech issues. Communicate this in your tech related queries to Cashfree. (optional) (default to undefined)
let x_idempotency_key: string; //An idempotency key is a unique identifier you include with your API call. If the request fails or times out, you can safely retry it using the same key to avoid duplicate actions.  (optional) (default to undefined)

const { status, data } = await apiInstance.pGSimulatePayment(
    x_api_version,
    SimulateRequest,
    x_request_id,
    x_idempotency_key
);
```

### Parameters

|Name | Type | Description  | Notes|
|------------- | ------------- | ------------- | -------------|
| **SimulateRequest** | **SimulateRequest**| Request body for simulation. | |
| **x_api_version** | [**string**] | API version to be used. Format is in YYYY-MM-DD. | defaults to '2026-01-01'|
| **x_request_id** | [**string**] | Request ID for the API call. Can be used to resolve tech issues. Communicate this in your tech related queries to Cashfree. | (optional) defaults to undefined|
| **x_idempotency_key** | [**string**] | An idempotency key is a unique identifier you include with your API call. If the request fails or times out, you can safely retry it using the same key to avoid duplicate actions.  | (optional) defaults to undefined|


### Return type

**SimulationResponse**

### Authorization

[XPartnerAPIKey](../README.md#XPartnerAPIKey), [XClientSecret](../README.md#XClientSecret), [XPartnerMerchantID](../README.md#XPartnerMerchantID), [XClientID](../README.md#XClientID), [XClientSignatureHeader](../README.md#XClientSignatureHeader)

### HTTP request headers

 - **Content-Type**: application/json
 - **Accept**: application/json

|-------------|-------------|------------------|
|**200** | Payment simulation response. |  * x-api-version -  <br>  * x-ratelimit-limit -  <br>  * x-ratelimit-remaining -  <br>  * x-ratelimit-retry -  <br>  * x-ratelimit-type -  <br>  * x-request-id -  <br>  |

# **pGSimulateSettlement**
> SettlementSimulationResponse pGSimulateSettlement(SimulateSettlementRequest)

Use this API to simulate a settlement in the sandbox environment. First, create a successful order, and then call this API with the order ID to simulate the settlement for that order.

### Example

```typescript
import {
    SimulationApi,
    Configuration,
    SimulateSettlementRequest
} from 'cashfree-pg';

const configuration = new Configuration();
const apiInstance = new SimulationApi(configuration);

let x_api_version: string; //API version to be used. Format is in YYYY-MM-DD. (default to '2026-01-01')
let SimulateSettlementRequest: SimulateSettlementRequest; //The request body for simulating a settlement.
let x_request_id: string; //Request ID for the API call. Can be used to resolve tech issues. Communicate this in your tech related queries to Cashfree. (optional) (default to undefined)
let x_idempotency_key: string; //An idempotency key is a unique identifier you include with your API call. If the request fails or times out, you can safely retry it using the same key to avoid duplicate actions.  (optional) (default to undefined)

const { status, data } = await apiInstance.pGSimulateSettlement(
    x_api_version,
    SimulateSettlementRequest,
    x_request_id,
    x_idempotency_key
);
```

### Parameters

|Name | Type | Description  | Notes|
|------------- | ------------- | ------------- | -------------|
| **SimulateSettlementRequest** | **SimulateSettlementRequest**| The request body for simulating a settlement. | |
| **x_api_version** | [**string**] | API version to be used. Format is in YYYY-MM-DD. | defaults to '2026-01-01'|
| **x_request_id** | [**string**] | Request ID for the API call. Can be used to resolve tech issues. Communicate this in your tech related queries to Cashfree. | (optional) defaults to undefined|
| **x_idempotency_key** | [**string**] | An idempotency key is a unique identifier you include with your API call. If the request fails or times out, you can safely retry it using the same key to avoid duplicate actions.  | (optional) defaults to undefined|


### Return type

**SettlementSimulationResponse**

### Authorization

 - **Content-Type**: application/json
 - **Accept**: application/json
